rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Use explicit type declarations in AGREE equations to match AADL data types.
Avoid using AADL property names directly as AGREE variables.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type contracts.
Generate range assumptions using named constants or visible feature names.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must be syntactically valid within the AGREE annex scope.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the expected data types of the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL timing properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE variables.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers unless the port is explicitly typed as bool.
irrelevant_topics: AADL component implementation details not related to visible features
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair actions
Model fusion and insertion location decisions
